reasoning: The document exhibits significant content loss and structural failures corroborated by both programmatic signals (word_count_ratio 0.725, shingling_recall 0.782) and LLM fidelity concerns. Two major sections are entirely missing, a 10-page content block is misordered at the end of the file, and the heading hierarchy is severely flattened. Furthermore, the failure of the content fidelity evaluation dimension creates an unacceptable gap in quality assurance that necessitates human intervention.

concerns.description: Fidelity Content evaluation failed — dimension not assessed
concerns.analysis: The content fidelity call did not produce valid results after retries. The document was not evaluated on this dimension.

The heading hierarchy is severely flattened in the output. Major sections and their subsections are represented as top-level sibling objects in the 'sections' array instead of using the 'subsections' property to reflect logical nesting. For example, under the heading 'NC Hydrography Issue, Gaps, and Recommendations' (index 8), subsections like 'Polyline Issues' (index 9) and 'Stream Segmentation' (index 10) are listed as siblings rather than children. This pattern continues throughout the issue
